channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Partner feels like an afterthought on his busy life calendar
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Partner feels like an afterthought on his busy life calendar

item.title: A newspaper in this city is the topic of today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: Hegseth forces ouster of senior Army spokesman in latest internal clash
item.description: It marks Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s latest move to force out an officer who worked for retired Gen. Mark A. Milley, a target of President Donald Trump.

item.title: Jesse Jackson. Iran talks. Epstein files. Slavery exhibit. Obama and aliens. Keto diet. Sleep and creativity. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Jesse Jackson, a leading African American voice on global stage, dies at 84
item.description: As a civil rights activist, he joined the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. in Selma, Alabama, and Memphis. He later launched two historic presidential campaigns.

item.title: The foods an oncologist recommends to lower cancer risk
item.description: Making a few changes to your diet can decrease your chance of developing cancer. Here’s what to eat — and what to avoid.

item.title: This flaw could prevent humans from becoming deep-space explorers
item.description: The flaw that could prevent humans from becoming deep-space explorers

item.title: Robert Duvall dies at 95. The Oscar-winning actor was a chameleon of the silver screen.
item.description: In “The Godfather” and “Apocalypse Now,” he became one of the most respected acting talents of his generation.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Does moving into boyfriend’s house mean following his rules?
item.description: Letter writer moved into boyfriend’s house despite a fear he might get controlling about “his” rules, which he did on Week 1.

item.title: The battle that’s tearing a small Virginia town apart
item.description: The drama in Purcellville involves a town council contingent that calls itself “Team Mayberry,” felony charges, lawsuits and an attempted citizen revolt.

item.title: An Olympic helmet in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Monday points.

item.title: Trump’s economy. Minnesota officers. Mitch McConnell. AI podcasts. Ocean current. Oncologist’s diet. Southwest. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: He spent decades perfecting his voice. Now he says Google stole it.
item.description: NPR’s David Greene says he was “completely freaked out” when he heard an AI voice that sounded just like his own. He’s suing over it, raising thorny questions for the courts to decide.

item.title: Trump is claiming victory on affordability, but public anxieties persist
item.description: Advisers had been encouraging the president to sound more empathetic toward struggling Americans. But as some bright spots emerge, the messaging has shifted.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: Why changes in a Florida ocean current could wreak havoc worldwide
item.description: It’s part of the scenario in “The Day After Tomorrow,” but scientists differ over whether or how fast it may occur. A team studying the current is working to bring clarity.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Daily crossword puzzles free from The Washington Post

item.title: Column: A new mortgage crisis is quietly hitting those who can least afford it
item.description: This week, there was yet another warning that many homeowners might be headed for trouble.

item.title: Researcher skeptical of ‘Havana syndrome’ tested secret weapon on himself — then suffered neurological symptoms, CIA was told
item.description: The bizarre story, described by four people familiar with the events, is the latest wrinkle in the decade-long quest to find the causes of Havana syndrome, the unexplained malady that has struck hundreds of U.S. spies and diplomats around the world.

item.title: ‘Highly likely’ that rare poison killed Putin nemesis Navalny, Europeans say
item.description: A toxin found in poison dart frogs probably killed Alexei Navalny in a Russian prison, five countries announced on the two-year anniversary of his death.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: New images of Trump’s ballroom show scale of structure that would match White House’s height
item.description: The planned 90,000-square-foot project would represent the most significant change to the White House grounds in decades. The project is advancing even as it is challenged in court and questioned on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Two ICE officers suspended after making ‘untruthful statements’ about shooting of Venezuelan man in Minneapolis last month
item.description: The federal immigration officers are facing a criminal inquiry after new evidence surfaced contradicting their accounts after the Jan. 14 shooting.

item.title: In Olympic shocker, Ilia Malinin finishes eighth in men’s figure skating
item.description: The self-proclaimed “Quadgod” fell multiple times during the free skate, and Kazakhstan’s Mikhail Shaidorov won the gold.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: My family wants me to take the fall for my dad’s crimes
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: My family wants me to take the fall for my dad’s crimes

item.title: White House, RFK Jr. shake up leadership at health department
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Department of Health and Human Services is undergoing a major overhaul ahead of this year’s midterm elections.

item.title: Au pair sentenced to 10 years for her role in Virginia double killing
item.description: Juliana Peres Magalhães, an au pair from Brazil, was working in Northern Virginia when police say she plotted with Brendan Banfield, who killed his wife and a stranger.

item.title: ICE plans to spend $38.3 billion turning warehouses into detention centers
item.description: Released documents offer the most complete picture to date of the Trump administration’s controversial and costly plan to overhaul immigrant detention using industrial buildings.

item.title: The Don Lemon indictment: Video appears to contradict key claims
item.description: The former CNN anchor, now an independent journalist, faces criminal charges after covering a protest at a church in St. Paul, Minnesota, last month.

item.title: Modern dating has a new double standard — thanks to AI. See for yourself.
item.description: I built a profile refiner to see what AI is doing to online dating.

item.title: 10 questions, and don't forget the bonus wager! It's your weekend edition of On the Record.
item.description: Answer now to try for a perfect score.

item.title: Inflation eased more than expected in January
item.description: Price growth cooled to a 2.4 percent annual rate, the slowest pace for inflation since May.

item.title: Greenhouse gases. Pete Hegseth. ICE operations. Nancy Guthrie. Cold winter. AI biases. Elephant secret. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: These are the states with the smartest, most beautiful, friendliest people — according to ChatGPT
item.description: Researchers uncovered hidden biases in ChatGPT’s assessment of people from different places. See how the chatbot ranked your city, state or country.

item.title: Judge halts Hegseth’s bid to punish Sen. Kelly for video message to troops
item.description: The Trump administration is expected to appeal the ruling, which says the retired Navy officer’s right to free speech is under attack.

item.title: Trump repeals landmark legal policy underpinning key climate rules
item.description: Nearly 17 years after the Environmental Protection Agency declared that carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases threaten the public’s health and welfare, the agency rescinded the legal opinion behind a wave of federal policies aimed at climate change.

item.title: In rare move, Congress exerts power over D.C. and blocks tax policy
item.description: City officials warn that the move could cost D.C. roughly $600 million in revenue through 2029 and cause major complications for tax season.

item.title: A consumption tax on Americans is brewing. Tariffs are the dry run.
item.description: When staggering entitlement costs finally come due, a desperate need for more revenue will kick in.

item.title: A ‘fighter’ in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: Border czar Tom Homan says Minnesota immigration surge is ending
item.description: The announcement comes after widespread protests and the killings of two U.S. citizens.

item.title: Pam Bondi. Flight stoppage. Trump rebuke. Tax data. James Van Der Beek. Olympics controversy. Happiness secret. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump will allow Democratic governors at White House meeting after initial snub
item.description: The president had initially excluded Democrats from the traditionally bipartisan meeting. However, not all Democrats were invited to a separate dinner to be held around the gathering.

item.title: James Van Der Beek dies at 48. He became a teen idol on ‘Dawson’s Creek' and battled colorectal cancer in recent years.
item.description: The ’90s teen drama vaulted him to a busy screen career that included self-deprecating comic roles and a performance as a high school quarterback in “Varsity Blues.”

item.title: Cutting off dad gave me peace. But sister has questions. Hax readers give advice.
item.description: Cutting off dad gave me peace. But sister has questions. Hax readers give advice.

item.title: The Trump administration has quietly withdrawn National Guard troops from Los Angeles, Chicago and Portland
item.description: The deployments encountered repeated legal setbacks that stymied President Donald Trump’s show of force in those cities.

item.title: JD Vance vies for the gold medal in coarseness and flippancy, George F. Will writes
item.description: The vice president has a knack for late — very late — adolescent naughtiness.

item.title: IRS improperly disclosed confidential immigrant tax data to DHS
item.description: The tax agency only recently discovered the mistake and is working with other federal agencies on a response.

item.title: This person’s travel schedule is the topic of today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Wednesday points.

item.title: FAA lifts restrictions at El Paso airport after short-lived ground stop for security reasons
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration reversed course Wednesday morning on a planned 10-day ground stop, saying air traffic could resume as normal. It did not provide a reason for the temporary closure.

item.title: U.S. employers added 130,000 jobs in January, the strongest gain in months
item.description: The unemployment rate ticked down to 4.3 percent, a slight improvement.
